packages.title: @vanrot/runtime
packages.summary: Signals, computed values, effects, batching, lifecycle helpers, DOM mounting, forms helpers, overlay helpers, and runtime graph contracts.

packages.title: @vanrot/compiler
packages.summary: Template compilation, scoped CSS rewriting, diagnostics, source maps, child components, slots, metadata readers, and generated component modules.

packages.title: @vanrot/config
packages.summary: Typed Vanrot config helpers, defaults, normalization, validation, migrations, diagnostics, and install-aware config edits.

packages.title: @vanrot/language-server
packages.summary: Vanrot template detection, LSP initialize handshake, stdio server entry, and IntelliJ-consumed template glob artifact.

packages.title: @vanrot/router
packages.summary: Route refs, layouts, nested outlets, navigation helpers, route metadata, preloading, keep-alive policies, and router diagnostics.

packages.title: @vanrot/vite-plugin
packages.summary: Vanrot component transforms, virtual modules, scoped CSS modules, diagnostics bridge, project metadata, and production builds.

packages.title: @vanrot/cli
packages.summary: Project creation, generation, UI additions, config inspection, doctor checks, project maps, AI rule files, dev, build, and test commands.

packages.title: @vanrot/ui
packages.summary: Shadcn-inspired Vanrot-native primitives used by the site and the component documentation catalog.

packages.title: @vanrot/testing
packages.summary: Readable component and workflow test helpers that stay lightweight until the post-production testing package phase.

packages.title: @vanrot/devtools
packages.summary: Project map graph contracts, DevTools panel assets, runtime graph metadata, and project intelligence helpers.

packages.title: @vanrot/ai
packages.summary: Generated AI knowledge bundle, verification, MCP server resources, Skill.sh package files, and provider-neutral framework rules.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/runtime *
publicExports.summary: All public exports from @vanrot/runtime are covered by the runtime and public API documentation.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/runtime signal
publicExports.summary: Creates a writable Vanrot signal for local state.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/runtime computed
publicExports.summary: Creates a derived value that tracks signal reads.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/runtime effect
publicExports.summary: Runs reactive side effects with cleanup and disposal.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/compiler *
publicExports.summary: All public exports from @vanrot/compiler are covered by compiler and public API documentation.

publicExports.summary: Compiles a Vanrot component source into executable output.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/compiler parseTemplate
publicExports.summary: Parses Vanrot template source into the compiler AST.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/compiler diagnosticCatalog
publicExports.summary: Exposes compiler diagnostic metadata for docs and tools.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/config *
publicExports.summary: All public exports from @vanrot/config are covered by configuration and public API documentation.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/config normalizeVanrotConfig
publicExports.summary: Normalizes config defaults and domains.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/config configDiagnosticCode
publicExports.summary: Lists config diagnostic codes.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/language-server *
publicExports.summary: All public exports from @vanrot/language-server are covered by editor language-server and public API documentation.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/language-server vanrotTemplateRules

publicExports.summary: All public exports from @vanrot/router are covered by routing and public API documentation.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/router defineRoutes

publicExports.title: @vanrot/router provideRouter
publicExports.summary: Installs router state into a Vanrot app.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/vite-plugin default
publicExports.summary: Default Vanrot Vite plugin export.

publicExports.summary: All public exports from @vanrot/cli are covered by CLI and public API documentation.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/cli createConsoleReporter
publicExports.summary: Creates the terminal reporter used by CLI commands.

publicExports.title: @vanrot/cli createMemoryReporter

publicExports.summary: All public exports from @vanrot/ui are covered by UI and public API documentation.

publicExports.summary: Structured UI component registry.

commands.title: vr create <project-name>
commands.summary: Creates a routed Vanrot app with the configured package manager and starter files.

commands.title: vr generate <kind> <name>
commands.summary: Generates convention-based component and page files.

commands.title: vr add <package-or-primitive>
commands.summary: Adds Vanrot packages or UI primitives and updates config when needed.

commands.title: vr ui <subcommand>
commands.summary: Inspects and manages Vanrot UI primitive metadata.

commands.title: vr config <subcommand>
commands.summary: Reads, validates, and reports normalized Vanrot config.

commands.title: vr doctor
commands.summary: Runs local project diagnostics with readable guidance.

commands.title: vr map
commands.summary: Writes the project intelligence manifest at `.vanrot/project-map.json`.

commands.title: vr init-ai
commands.summary: Writes provider-neutral project rules for AI assistants from normalized config.

commands.title: vr ai <subcommand>
commands.summary: Builds, verifies, and inspects AI-readable Vanrot knowledge alongside project AI doorway files.

commands.title: vr dev
commands.summary: Starts the configured Vite development server through the Vanrot CLI.

commands.title: vr build
commands.summary: Runs a production build through the Vanrot CLI.

commands.title: vr test
commands.summary: Runs the configured test command for a Vanrot project.

diagnostics.title: VR001
diagnostics.summary: Compiler diagnostic for unsupported or invalid component source.

diagnostics.title: VR019
diagnostics.summary: Compiler diagnostic for the final current compiler catalog entry.

diagnostics.title: VRCFG001
diagnostics.summary: Config diagnostic for invalid config shape or loading failure.

diagnostics.title: VRCFG008
diagnostics.summary: Config diagnostic for the Phase 13 validation catalog.

diagnostics.title: VRCFG009
diagnostics.summary: Config diagnostic for AI rule section validation.

diagnostics.title: VRCFG010
diagnostics.summary: Config diagnostic for AI rule ordering validation.

diagnostics.title: VRCFG011
diagnostics.summary: Config diagnostic for AI rule customization validation.

diagnostics.title: VRCFG012
diagnostics.summary: Config diagnostic for AI rule source validation.

diagnostics.title: VR_CHILD_BEFORE_PARENT
diagnostics.summary: Router diagnostic for child routes declared before a valid parent.

generatedFiles.title: package.json
generatedFiles.summary: Project manifest containing scripts, dependencies, package manager metadata, and private workspace state.

generatedFiles.title: tsconfig.json
generatedFiles.summary: TypeScript project configuration generated for Vanrot app source files and package resolution.

generatedFiles.title: vite.config.ts
generatedFiles.summary: Vite configuration that installs the Vanrot plugin.

generatedFiles.title: vanrot.config.ts
generatedFiles.summary: Typed Vanrot config source for source roots, dev server defaults, UI configuration, and AI rule settings.

generatedFiles.title: src/routes.ts
generatedFiles.summary: Named route refs and route metadata for app navigation without repeated route strings.

generatedFiles.title: .vanrot/project-map.json
generatedFiles.summary: Project intelligence manifest generated by `vr map` for devtools and local inspection.

conventions.title: Role suffixes
conventions.summary: Components, pages, dialogs, layouts, widgets, and forms use explicit role-based file suffixes.

conventions.title: Scoped CSS
conventions.summary: Component styles live in sibling scoped CSS files instead of global style buckets.

conventions.title: Signals for state
conventions.summary: Vanrot application state uses signals and readable helpers instead of nested imperative flow.

conventions.title: Route refs
conventions.summary: Routes use named refs and central sources of truth instead of repeated path strings.

conventions.title: No UI markup in TypeScript
conventions.summary: UI markup belongs in HTML templates, while TypeScript owns state, imports, and behavior.

components.title: Button
components.summary: Use buttons for actions and command triggers. Usage: <vr-button variant.default type="button">Save</vr-button> Accessibility: Buttons lower to native button elements and preserve focus, disabled, keyboard, and type behavior.

components.title: Card
components.summary: Use cards for grouped content surfaces. Usage: <vr-card variant.default>Content</vr-card> Accessibility: Cards lower to article-like surfaces and keep child content readable.

components.title: Badge
components.summary: Use badges for short status, category, or metadata labels. Usage: <vr-badge tone.success>Ready</vr-badge> Accessibility: Badge examples should not rely on color alone for meaning.

components.title: Avatar
components.summary: Use avatars for person, tenant, team, or entity identity. Usage: <vr-avatar variant.soft>VR</vr-avatar> Accessibility: Avatar content should include accessible names when it represents a person or entity.

components.title: Alert
components.summary: Use alerts for persistent inline feedback. Usage: <vr-alert tone.info>Project ready.</vr-alert> Accessibility: Alerts use severity-aware semantics without making every note interruptive.

components.title: Loader
components.summary: Use loaders for pending activity. Usage: <vr-loader variant.spinner aria-label="Loading"></vr-loader> Accessibility: Decorative loaders are hidden by default unless labelled.

components.title: Skeleton
components.summary: Use skeletons to preserve layout while content loads. Usage: <vr-skeleton variant.text></vr-skeleton> Accessibility: Skeletons are decorative by default and should not replace real loading labels.

components.title: Separator
components.summary: Use separators to divide related content. Usage: <vr-separator orientation.horizontal></vr-separator> Accessibility: Separators expose orientation when semantics are needed.

components.title: Layout
components.summary: Use layouts for full-page shell structure. Usage: <vr-layout><vr-router></vr-router></vr-layout> Accessibility: Layouts preserve landmarks provided by child header, main, sidebar, and footer regions.

components.title: Container
components.summary: Use containers to constrain readable content width. Usage: <vr-container size.lg>Content</vr-container> Accessibility: Containers are structural and should not replace semantic headings or landmarks.

components.title: Section
components.summary: Use sections to separate meaningful page regions. Usage: <vr-section spacing.md>Content</vr-section> Accessibility: Sections should include clear headings when they introduce a new content region.

components.title: Grid
components.summary: Use grids for responsive column layouts. Usage: <vr-grid cols.3 gap.4>Content</vr-grid> Accessibility: Grids control layout only; keep reading order meaningful in the source.

components.title: Header
components.summary: Use headers for top-level page or section identity. Usage: <vr-header>Header</vr-header> Accessibility: Headers lower to native header landmarks when used in page structure.

components.title: Footer
components.summary: Use footers for closing page or section metadata. Usage: <vr-footer>Footer</vr-footer> Accessibility: Footers lower to native footer landmarks when used in page structure.

components.title: Sidebar
components.summary: Use sidebars for static adjacent navigation or secondary content. Usage: <vr-sidebar placement.left>Navigation</vr-sidebar> Accessibility: Sidebars lower to aside and should be paired with labelled navigation when they contain links.

components.title: Nav
components.summary: Use nav for labelled navigation groups. Usage: <vr-nav aria-label="Primary">Links</vr-nav> Accessibility: Navigation elements must keep clear aria-label values when multiple nav regions are present.

components.title: Breadcrumb
components.summary: Use breadcrumbs to show the current page path. Usage: <vr-breadcrumb aria-label="Breadcrumb">Path</vr-breadcrumb> Accessibility: Breadcrumbs lower to nav and should expose the current page in the trail.

components.title: Image
components.summary: Use images for responsive visual media. Usage: <vr-img src="/image.png" alt="Preview"></vr-img> Accessibility: Images preserve platform alt text requirements; decorative images should use an empty alt value.

components.title: Source
components.summary: Use sources inside picture or media elements. Usage: <vr-src srcset="/image.avif" type="image/avif"></vr-src> Accessibility: Sources are media candidates and rely on the parent media element for accessible text.

components.title: Form
components.summary: Use forms to group registered controls and submit application-owned data. Usage: <vr-form><vr-form-field name="email" required><vr-input type.email></vr-input></vr-form-field></vr-form> Accessibility: Submit orchestration marks invalid fields touched before preventing submit.

components.title: Form Field
components.summary: Use form fields to bind a label, help text, validation message, and control. Usage: <vr-form-field name="email" label="Email"><vr-input name="email"></vr-input></vr-form-field> Accessibility: Connect labels, descriptions, and validation messages to the control with stable field names.

components.title: Label
components.summary: Use labels for visible control names that can target a native input. Usage: <vr-label for="email">Email</vr-label> Accessibility: Labels should use for when targeting a separate control and remain visible for editable fields.

components.title: Input
components.summary: Use inputs for single-line form values with typed dotted tokens. Usage: <vr-input type.email size.md name="email" placeholder="Email"></vr-input> Accessibility: Use a visible <vr-label> or aria-label. Validation sets aria-invalid and aria-describedby when an error element is present.

components.title: Textarea
components.summary: Use textareas for multi-line form values. Usage: <vr-textarea name="notes" placeholder="Notes"></vr-textarea> Accessibility: Pair each textarea with a visible label or aria-label and connect help text through aria-describedby.

components.title: Select
components.summary: Use selects for choosing one value from application-owned options. Usage: <vr-select name="status"><option>Draft</option><option>Ready</option></vr-select> Accessibility: Keep a visible label or aria-label and preserve native option semantics.

components.title: Checkbox
components.summary: Use checkboxes for independent boolean choices. Usage: <vr-checkbox name="accepted" checked></vr-checkbox> Accessibility: Checkboxes keep native checked state; pair each control with an accessible label.

components.title: Radio Group
components.summary: Use radio groups to collect mutually exclusive choices. Usage: <vr-radio-group name="plan"><vr-radio value="basic"></vr-radio><vr-radio value="pro"></vr-radio></vr-radio-group> Accessibility: Use a group label and keep radio options keyboard reachable.

components.title: Radio
components.summary: Use radios for one option inside a shared group. Usage: <vr-radio name="plan" value="pro" checked></vr-radio> Accessibility: Radios keep native checked state and should share a name with related options.

components.title: Switch
components.summary: Use switches for immediate on/off settings. Usage: <vr-switch checked aria-label="Notifications"></vr-switch> Accessibility: Compiler lowering keeps switch as a button with role switch.

components.title: Slider
components.summary: Use sliders for bounded numeric values. Usage: <vr-slider name="volume" min="0" max="100" step="5"></vr-slider> Accessibility: Expose a label and keep min, max, step, and value understandable to assistive technology.

components.title: Table
components.summary: Use tables for structured data with optional sorting, filtering, pagination, and selection state. Usage: <vr-table density.compact sortable filterable paginated selectable></vr-table> Accessibility: Use <vr-table-caption> for accessible context when visible title copy helps.

components.title: Table Header
components.summary: Use table headers to group heading rows. Usage: <vr-table-header><vr-table-row><vr-table-head>Name</vr-table-head></vr-table-row></vr-table-header> Accessibility: Table headers lower to thead and should contain header cells for column context.

components.title: Table Body
components.summary: Use table bodies to group data rows. Usage: <vr-table-body><vr-table-row><vr-table-cell>Value</vr-table-cell></vr-table-row></vr-table-body> Accessibility: Table bodies lower to tbody and should keep rows aligned with table headers.

components.title: Table Row
components.summary: Use table rows inside table header, body, or footer groups. Usage: <vr-table-row selected><vr-table-cell>Value</vr-table-cell></vr-table-row> Accessibility: Rows can expose selected or disabled state while preserving native row structure.

components.title: Table Head
components.summary: Use table head cells for column or row headings. Usage: <vr-table-head sort="name">Name</vr-table-head> Accessibility: Header cells lower to th and should describe their related table cells.

components.title: Table Cell
components.summary: Use table cells for regular tabular values. Usage: <vr-table-cell>Value</vr-table-cell> Accessibility: Cells lower to td and should stay aligned with row and column headers.

components.title: Table Footer
components.summary: Use table footers for totals, summaries, or repeated table actions. Usage: <vr-table-footer><vr-table-row><vr-table-cell>Total</vr-table-cell></vr-table-row></vr-table-footer> Accessibility: Footers lower to tfoot and should not replace row or column headers.

components.title: Table Caption
components.summary: Use table captions for concise table context. Usage: <vr-table-caption>Recent invoices</vr-table-caption> Accessibility: Captions lower to caption and should describe the table content.

components.title: Pagination
components.summary: Use pagination for navigating paged data sets. Usage: <vr-pagination page="1" total="120" pageSize="20"></vr-pagination> Accessibility: Pagination lowers to nav and should include a useful aria-label when multiple nav regions exist.

components.title: List
components.summary: Use lists for ordered or unordered grouped content. Usage: <vr-list marker.disc><vr-list-item>One</vr-list-item><vr-list-item>Two</vr-list-item></vr-list> Accessibility: Lists keep native list semantics; choose marker and density without removing item meaning.

components.title: List Item
components.summary: Use list items inside Vanrot list primitives. Usage: <vr-list-item>Checklist item</vr-list-item> Accessibility: List items lower to li and should remain children of a list.

components.title: Stat
components.summary: Use stats for compact metric readouts with tone and alignment tokens. Usage: <vr-stat tone.success align.left>98%</vr-stat> Accessibility: Stats should keep labels close to values so the metric is understandable out of context.

components.title: Empty State
components.summary: Use empty states when a data view has no available records. Usage: <vr-empty-state tone.muted>No records yet</vr-empty-state> Accessibility: Empty states should explain the missing content and expose recovery actions when available.

components.title: Dialog
components.summary: Use dialogs for focused modal decisions, edits, or confirmations. Usage: <vr-dialog size.md><vr-dialog-trigger>Open</vr-dialog-trigger><vr-dialog-content>Content</vr-dialog-content></vr-dialog> Accessibility: Dialogs move focus into content when opened and restore focus to the trigger when closed.

components.title: Drawer
components.summary: Use drawers for temporary side panels that keep users in the current page context. Usage: <vr-drawer side.right><vr-drawer-trigger>Open</vr-drawer-trigger><vr-drawer-content>Content</vr-drawer-content></vr-drawer> Accessibility: Drawers use the shared overlay controller, close on escape or outside pointer, and preserve focus return.

components.title: Dropdown
components.summary: Use dropdowns for compact action lists and contextual menus. Usage: <vr-dropdown align.end side.bottom><vr-dropdown-trigger>Account</vr-dropdown-trigger><vr-dropdown-content><vr-dropdown-item>Profile</vr-dropdown-item></vr-dropdown-content></vr-dropdown> Accessibility: Dropdown triggers remain keyboard reachable and content closes predictably through the overlay controller.

components.title: Tabs
components.summary: Use tabs for switching between related panels without leaving the page. Usage: <vr-tabs value.overview><vr-tabs-list><vr-tabs-trigger value.overview>Overview</vr-tabs-trigger></vr-tabs-list><vr-tabs-panel value.overview>Overview</vr-tabs-panel></vr-tabs> Accessibility: Tabs wire tab and tabpanel roles, selected state, focus order, and arrow-key movement through a small controller.

components.title: Toast
components.summary: Use toasts for short, non-blocking feedback messages. Usage: <vr-toast tone.success><vr-toast-title>Saved</vr-toast-title><vr-toast-description>Changes were saved.</vr-toast-description></vr-toast> Accessibility: Toast queues should use a labelled viewport and keep messages dismissible without stealing focus.

components.title: Popover
components.summary: Use popovers for anchored contextual panels that keep users in the current workflow. Usage: <vr-popover align.center side.bottom><vr-popover-trigger><vr-button variant.outline>Open popover</vr-button></vr-popover-trigger><vr-popover-content><vr-popover-title>Dimensions</vr-popover-title><input value="100%" /></vr-popover-content></vr-popover> Accessibility: Popovers return focus to their trigger, close on escape or outside pointer, and keep labelled content available to assistive technology.

components.title: Tooltip
components.summary: Use tooltips for short supporting copy on compact controls. Usage: <vr-tooltip side.top align.center><vr-tooltip-trigger><vr-button variant.outline>Hover</vr-button></vr-tooltip-trigger><vr-tooltip-content>Add to library</vr-tooltip-content></vr-tooltip> Accessibility: Tooltips supplement the trigger label and should not hide information needed to complete the task.

components.title: Command Menu
components.summary: Use command menus for searchable keyboard-first action and navigation surfaces. Usage: <vr-command-menu density.compact><vr-command-menu-input placeholder="Type a command or search..."></vr-command-menu-input><vr-command-menu-group><vr-command-menu-item value.calendar>Calendar</vr-command-menu-item></vr-command-menu-group></vr-command-menu> Accessibility: Command menus keep active descendant state, skip disabled items, and expose selectable actions through predictable keyboard movement.

routes.summary: Vanrot - Framework Documentation And Design Components Vanrot's public landing page for framework documentation, design components, examples, and production-readiness notes.

routes.title: /docs
routes.summary: Vanrot Framework Documentation Framework documentation for Vanrot packages, APIs, commands, diagnostics, conventions, examples, limitations, and deployment preparation.

routes.title: /docs/components
routes.summary: Vanrot Design Component Catalog Design component documentation for Vanrot's October UI primitives, variants, usage examples, and API notes.

limitations.title: Post-production features are not current framework behavior
limitations.summary: Brutalist UI, the expanded testing package, store, forms and async primitives, and SSR or hydration remain outside current public framework behavior.

limitations.title: Deployment is prepared, not launched
limitations.summary: The site is prepared for production hosting, but DNS, credentials, analytics setup, and live deployment are outside this phase.

deployment.title: vanrot.vankode.com
deployment.summary: The site is prepared for production hosting, but DNS, credentials, analytics, and live deployment are not performed by this repository phase.

docs.title: Vanrot
docs.summary: Vanrot is a small frontend framework with compiler-known templates, signals, route ownership, source-owned UI, and a documentation-first development path.

docs.title: Installation
docs.summary: Create or run a Vanrot app through the CLI and workspace package graph.

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.summary: Vanrot keeps source roles explicit with TypeScript, HTML, and CSS side by side.

docs.title: Runtime
docs.summary: The runtime owns signals, computed values, effects, lifecycle hooks, inputs, and mounting.

docs.title: Compiler
docs.summary: The compiler turns role files into mounted DOM with scoped CSS, diagnostics, source maps, control flow, child components, slots, and UI primitive lowering.

docs.title: Vite Plugin
docs.summary: The Vite plugin compiles Vanrot role files in dev and build, bridges diagnostics, watches sibling files, and emits source maps.

docs.title: CLI
docs.summary: The CLI provides project creation, generation, UI primitive add flows, dev/build/test wrappers, doctor, map, config maintenance, and AI-readable local context.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.summary: vanrot.config.ts is the typed source of truth for source roots, dev server port, UI flavor, UI style mode, and future package domains.

docs.title: Routing
docs.summary: The router owns route refs, nested layouts, params, query strings, redirects, guards, active links, breadcrumbs, preloading, and keepAlive.

docs.title: UI October
docs.summary: October is Vanrot's dark-first, light-capable UI foundation with source-owned primitives, tokens, and vanrotstyles.

docs.title: Theming
docs.summary: Vanrot themes use CSS custom properties for colors, surfaces, radius, shadows, typography, motion, and z-index layers.

docs.title: vanrotstyles
docs.summary: vanrotstyles.css is Vanrot's first-party utility CSS layer with unprefixed utility classes.

docs.title: Testing
docs.summary: Vanrot testing currently provides component test helpers and will expand after the production UI and site base are in place.

docs.title: Devtools And Project Intelligence
docs.summary: Use Vanrot project intelligence, graph metadata, and devtools contracts without relying on AI-specific bundles from Phase 25.

docs.title: Examples
docs.summary: Examples show practical Vanrot usage without hiding the source files.

docs.title: Runnable Example Matrix
docs.summary: Use verified example workspaces as the source of truth for framework workflows and docs snippets.

docs.title: Build And Deployment Preparation
docs.summary: Prepare a Vanrot site for production hosting without pretending this repository controls DNS, credentials, analytics, or live deployment.

docs.title: Public API Reference
docs.summary: Read the documented public exports for each current Vanrot package and see which guide owns the behavior.

docs.title: Diagnostics Reference
docs.summary: Find the current compiler, config, router, CLI, and Vite-plugin diagnostic codes with user-facing explanations.

docs.title: Generated Files And Directories
docs.summary: Understand generated files Vanrot users should expect in starter apps, config flows, route workflows, and project intelligence output.

docs.title: October Showcase
docs.summary: A broad Phase 16G composition surface for admin, dashboard, and mobile patterns.

docs.title: Conventions
docs.summary: Vanrot conventions keep projects readable for humans and AI tools.

docs.title: Limitations And Deferred Work
docs.summary: Read honest status notes for demo-capable, limited, deferred, and not-browser-facing areas before using Vanrot in production contexts.

docs.title: Reference Status
docs.summary: The reference section shows what is available now, demo-capable, in progress, planned, or deferred.
